old_claim.note: Newspaper Periodicals provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim_absent: No newspaper prerequisite remains because weekly Amsterdam price-currents began around 1585 while the same study dates Amsterdam's printed newspapers from about 1618.

source_shape.source_locator: Pages 7-8 distinguish printed commodity price-currents beginning around 1585 from printed Amsterdam newspapers beginning around 1618.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The study establishes separate publication traditions and places price-currents roughly three decades before local printed newspapers.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: Shared use of a press does not make the later newspaper format causal infrastructure for the earlier specialized price sheet.

ontology_before: Specialized market price sheets were modeled as depending on a later general-news periodical format.
ontology_after: Price-currents and newspapers are parallel print products with distinct documented start dates.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: printed_financial_price_lists no longer directly depends on newspaper_periodicals.
would_reject_if: Evidence dated a defining newspaper-hosted price-list format earlier than the independent 1585 Amsterdam commodity sheets.
short_reason: Amsterdam price-currents predate Amsterdam printed newspapers by about three decades.
